column1: Paul and his wife Sharon were out shopping at a local shopping centre. They______________their car in the car park and were now returning to it with their shopping bags. (PARK)
Paul put the car key into the door, but it wouldn't turn. "What's wrong with this key?" he asked Sharon. "It_____________to be working." (NOT SEEM)

Confused, Sharon stepped back and took a good look at the car. "You know what, Paul? I don't think this car is_____________. It just looks exactly like it. We're trying to het in someone else's car!" They walked away and tired to remember where they'd parked their own car!" (WE)

The City of Westminster is the borough of London that contains Buckingham Place, the Houses of Parliament and the Prime Minister's residence. It is located on the northern banks of the River Thames in the centre of London, and it's one of the________________places to live in the UK. (EXPENSIVE)
The borough is of great historical interest, and almost four fifths of_____________buildings are part of a designed conservation area. (IT)
Of all the tourists that visit London, approximately 95% of them tour the City of Westminister, which___________for about 28 million visitors each year. (ACCOUNT)

Other sites within the brought are Piccadilly Circus, Kensington Gardens, te headquarters of many global corporations and several renowned learning institutes. The borough has 27 tube stations and______________to south London by seven bridges. (CONNECT)
